data.title: Meta Platforms Considers Layoffs in Reality Labs Division
data.description: According to Odaily, Meta Platforms is contemplating layoffs within its Reality Labs division, specifically targeting the branch involved in 'metaverse' operations. Three informed employees revealed that the company might initiate these layoffs as early as next month, potentially affecting 10% to 30% of the team. This team primarily focuses on VR headsets and VR-based social networks. Reality Labs comprises the metaverse and wearable devices departments. Sources indicate that management plans to redirect the savings from these layoffs towards the AR glasses project. Meta launched AR glasses in collaboration with Ray-Ban in 2021, surpassing internal sales targets. Insiders report that Meta is reassessing its investments in the metaverse, with executives considering significant cuts to VR-related positions within the metaverse department.

data.title: BlackRock Chief Investment Officer Rick Rieder believes the Federal Reserve seats are independent.
data.description: Rick Rieder, chief investment officer at BlackRock and a potential candidate for Federal Reserve chair, believes the Fed seat is independent.

data.title: Meta plans to cut investment in its Metaverse team and redirect funds to its VR glasses business.
data.description: According to The New York Times, three employees familiar with the matter said that Meta Platforms (META.O) is considering layoffs in a branch of its Reality Labs division that works on the "metaverse" business, potentially starting as early as next month, affecting 10% to 30% of the team's employees. This team is primarily responsible for VR headsets and VR-based social networks. Reality Labs comprises the Metaverse and Wearables divisions. Sources indicate that management expects to redirect the savings from the layoffs to an AR glasses project. Meta launched AR glasses in 2021 in partnership with Ray-Ban, and sales have exceeded internal targets. The sources say that Meta is reassessing its metaverse investments, and within the metaverse division, executives are considering significant cuts to VR-related positions.

data.title: White House: Diplomacy is the U.S.'s "preferred" solution to the situation in Iran.
data.description: White House Press Secretary Janet Levitt said in a media interview on the 12th that, facing the current situation in Iran, US President Trump considers diplomacy the "preferred" option. Levitt said Trump "has consistently emphasized that diplomacy is the preferred option," but if he deems it necessary, he "will not hesitate to use the US military." Trump said on the 11th that Iran had contacted US government officials and proposed negotiations, and that "meetings are being arranged." Meanwhile, the US is considering a range of measures to address the situation in Iran, including "some very tough options" such as military action. Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi stated at a meeting of foreign diplomats in Tehran on the 12th that Iran is prepared to negotiate with the US on the basis of "mutual respect," but is also prepared for war.

data.title: Wall Street Journal: The White House weighs its Iran nuclear proposal, advising Trump to "try diplomacy first, then force."
data.description: According to the Wall Street Journal, U.S. officials say the White House is weighing Iran's final diplomatic engagement proposal to limit its nuclear program, even as Trump currently leans toward authorizing a new military strike against Iran. Officials say some senior government aides, led by Vice President Vance, are urging Trump to try diplomatic means before “retaliating” against Iran’s actions in the unrest.

data.title: German Foreign Minister Optimistic About U.S.-Europe Compromise on Greenland Issue
data.description: According to Odaily, German Foreign Minister Johann Wadeful expressed optimism about Europe's ability to reach a compromise with the United States regarding the Greenland issue. After meeting with U.S. Secretary of State Rubio in Washington on Monday, Wadeful stated his hope for U.S. involvement in a new NATO mission in the Arctic region to help ensure Greenland's security. Wadeful remarked, "NATO is currently beginning to formulate specific plans, which will be discussed with our American partners. Although we have not completed this step today, all parties are prepared to work within the NATO framework. Germany will also strive to contribute to this effort." Wadeful also discussed ongoing negotiations between the U.S., Europe, and Ukraine with Rubio. Germany has clearly indicated its willingness to support a military mission as part of security assurances.

data.description: White House Press Secretary Levitt: Airstrikes against Iran are one of many options.

data.title: Bessant warns: The Fed investigation could trigger market turmoil and lead Powell to refuse to relinquish power.
data.description: According to AXIOS, two sources familiar with the matter revealed that U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessant told Trump Sunday evening that the federal investigation into the Federal Reserve Chairman had "made a mess" and could have adverse effects on financial markets. One source familiar with the call between Bessant and Trump said, "The secretary was very unhappy, and he's let the president know about it." Trump appears to be deliberately distancing himself from the investigation into Fed Chairman Powell. The source said that the Washington office of U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro launched the investigation without prior notification to the Treasury Department, senior White House officials, or the Justice Department. Another source said that Bessant "thought that Powell would leave when the president appointed a new Fed chairman. But that's not happening now. Now Powell is taking a tougher stance. It's really made a mess of things." However, the source said that Bessant did not question the necessity of investigating Powell in his conversation with Trump, nor was he defending the Fed chairman.

data.title: Trump: Any country doing business with Iran will be subject to a 25% tariff.
data.description: US President Trump: Effective immediately, any country doing business with Iran will be subject to a 25% tariff on all its commercial activities with the United States. This order is final and binding.

data.title: U.S. Small-Cap Stocks Achieve Longest Winning Streak in Seven Years
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, U.S. small-cap stocks have reached a significant milestone, marking their longest winning streak against large-cap stocks in seven years. On Monday, the Russell 2000 Index closed at a record high, extending its consecutive victories over the S&P 500 Index to seven days. The last time a longer winning streak occurred was in January 2019, when the stock market was recovering from a sharp decline.

data.title: Helius Labs’ CEO gives Solana edge in AI benefits over EVM networks
data.description:
The CEO of Helius Labs, Mert Mumtaz, whose company provides infrastructure and tooling for Solana developers, stated in a post on X that Solana’s program model is fundamentally safer for AI development than the interface model used by Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM)-based blockchains. He also predicted the emergence of several billion-dollar startups on Solana this […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/helius-labs-ceo-gives-solana-edge-in-ai/
